text: The surgeon performs a transanal submucosal dissection for a large lower rectal adenoma using the Thunderbit tissue management system.
The patient was a eighty-six years old female, complained of anal discomfort with intermittent bleeding for two months.
The digital rectal exam and colonoscope reveal a well-defined tumor approximately four centimeters in size, with a soft consistency, extending from the dentin line to the lower rectum.
Biopsy revealed adenoma with low-grade dysplasia.
The surgeon performs submucosal dissection of a large lower rectal adenoma using a traditional trans-anal approach, assisted by the Thunderbit tissue management system.
The surgeon uses an anal retractor to clearly expose the margin of the tumor, revealing its well-defined edges within the lower rectum.
The surgeon injects normal saline mixed with 2% lidocaine around the tumor to elevate the submucosa, enhancing the visibility and accessibility of the tumor margins.
The surgeon uses a scalpel to mark the lowest dissection margin from the dentin line, preparing for further surgical intervention.
The surgeon uses the Thunderbit device to perform submucosal dissection around the tumor, continuing the trans-anal approach.
The surgeon identifies the dissection plane with ease, using the Thunderbit device to achieve effective hemostasis throughout the procedure.
The anal sphincter muscle is visible in the surgical field, exposed using an anal retractor.
The surgeon continues the dissection up to the lower rectum, following the previously marked dissection margin and elevated submucosa around the tumor.
The surgeon continues the dissection with a clear view of the dissection plane, utilizing the established approach.
The surgeon ligates the pedicle of the tumor using a clamp, securing the structure within the surgical field.
The surgeon uses the Thunderbit device to transect the pedicle of the tumor, following its previous ligation.
The surgeon suture ligates the stump using 3-0 Vicryl, securing the tissue with visible stitches.
The whole procedure was performed smoothly within thirty minutes with minimal blood loss.
The patient was discharged one day after the surgery.
The final pathology proved cecil serrated adenoma with free surgical margin.
